title: Loranthella
taxonomy: Loranthella is classified within the family Loranthaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as mistletoes. This family comprises about 73 genera and over 1,000 species. Loranthaceae are hemiparasitic plants, meaning they obtain some of their nutrients by connecting to the vascular system of other plants. The Loranthella genus, specifically, is endemic to Australia and contains several species. Further classification within the genus involves specific characteristics and morphological features of each species.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
